name: Aguarales de Valdemilaz - Piedratajada - Marracos — from Valpalmas
name_original: Aguarales de Valdemilaz - Piedratajada - Marracos — desde Valpalmas
region_intro: Touring cycling around Valpalmas, located in the Cinco Villas region of Zaragoza, Spain, offers routes through varied natural landscapes. The area is characterized by flat-topped hills, cereal lands, and unique geological formations such as the Aguarales de Valpalmas, which feature eroded clay structures. The broader Zaragoza province provides a mosaic of terrains, including river valleys and arid steppes, suitable for diverse cycling experiences. This region is actively developing and promoting cycling, providing a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Valpalmas The most popular touring cycling route is Castillos de Obano y Yéquera — circular from Monasterio de la Virgen del Monlora, a 19.7 miles (31.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 28 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area's historical castles and natural surroundings. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Aguarales de Valdemilaz - Piedratajada - Marracos — from Valpalmas, a moderate 17.2 miles (27.7 km) path. This route passes through the distinctive Aguarales de Valpalmas, known for their unique geological formations. Local touring cyclists also love the Sotonera Reservoir – La Sotonera Reservoir loop from Puendeluna, a 29.7 miles (47.7 km) trail leading through landscapes featuring the Sotonera Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Valpalmas is defined by varied topography, including flat-topped hills, cereal fields, and unique geological formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 38 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Valpalmas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Valpalmas?
A: Touring cycling around Valpalmas offers a diverse topography. You can expect routes that traverse flat-topped hills, extensive cereal lands, and unique geological formations like the Aguarales de Valpalmas. The broader Zaragoza province also features a mosaic of landscapes, including river valleys and arid steppes, providing a varied cycling experience.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Valpalmas area?
A: There are 10 touring cycling routes available around Valpalmas, offering a range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are categorized by difficulty, with 5 moderate and 5 difficult options to choose from.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes near Valpalmas?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994065696/castillos-de-obano-y-yequera-circular-from-monasterio-de-la-virgen-del-monlora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillos de Obano y Yéquera — circular from Monasterio de la Virgen del Monlora&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 31.8 km trail exploring historical castles and natural surroundings. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38183653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotonera Reservoir – La Sotonera Reservoir loop from Puendeluna&lt;/a&gt;, a 47.7 km moderate route.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions I can see while cycling near Valpalmas?
A: The most striking natural feature is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz&lt;/a&gt;, known for its unique geological formations resembling 'fairy chimneys' or a lunar landscape. You can experience these formations on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993999641/aguarales-de-valdemilaz-piedratajada-marracos-from-valpalmas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguarales de Valdemilaz - Piedratajada - Marracos — from Valpalmas&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5857443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ardisa Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the Sotonera Reservoir offer scenic views along other routes.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers a blend of nature and culture. You can encounter historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yéquera Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994065696/castillos-de-obano-y-yequera-circular-from-monasterio-de-la-virgen-del-monlora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillos de Obano y Yéquera&lt;/a&gt; route. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3716853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Monlora and Viewpoint of the Cinco Villas&lt;/a&gt; also provides cultural interest and panoramic views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Valpalmas?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the region's climate suggests spring and autumn would be ideal. These seasons typically offer milder temperatures, making for more comfortable cycling conditions compared to the potentially hot summer months or cooler winter periods.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available in the Valpalmas area?
A: The longest touring cycling route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31507339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel Castle – Ardisa Dam loop from Alda de Monlora&lt;/a&gt;. This challenging route spans 101.2 km and typically takes around 6 hours and 30 minutes to complete, offering significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the difficulty level of touring cycling routes around Valpalmas?
A: The touring cycling routes around Valpalmas cater to different ability levels. There are 5 moderate routes and 5 difficult routes available, ensuring options for both experienced cyclists and those looking for a more challenging ride.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Valpalmas?
A: Cyclists frequently praise the varied topography, which includes flat-topped hills, cereal fields, and unique geological formations. The region's active promotion of cycling tourism and its blend of natural beauty and cultural interest also contribute to a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Ardisa Dam?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31507339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel Castle – Ardisa Dam loop from Alda de Monlora&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31506791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Gállego River – Ardisa Dam loop from Puendeluna&lt;/a&gt; are two difficult routes that feature the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5857443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ardisa Dam&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views of this man-made landmark.

Q: Is Valpalmas a good destination for touring cyclists seeking diverse landscapes?
A: Absolutely. Valpalmas and the broader Cinco Villas region are characterized by a wide range of landscapes, from the unique geological formations of the Aguarales to flat river valleys and arid steppes. This diversity provides an engaging backdrop for touring cyclists looking for varied scenery.
